title: Payback-Period Architecture

content.statement: Growth quality depends on how fast acquisition spend returns as gross profit.
content.question: What's your business's target CAC payback, and are you on track to hit it at your market launch?
content.equation: PPA = CAC ÷ Monthly Gross Profit per Acquired Customer
sop: Calculate your average CAC per acquisition channel (see calculation, page 6).
Calculate Monthly Gross Profit per Acquired Customer, revenue minus direct costs, divided by months (see calculation, page 6).
Divide CAC by Monthly Gross Profit to get your payback period in months for each channel (see calculation, page 6).
Set a maximum acceptable payback period (commonly 6-12 months) and rank your channels against it.
Pause or renegotiate spend on any channel whose payback period exceeds your maximum.
content.explainer: Long payback compounds risk. Short payback compounds freedom.
questions: What's your business's target CAC payback, and are you on track to hit it at your market launch?
If it takes 8 months to earn back what you spent acquiring a customer, what happens to your growth plans if 3 of those months go quiet?
Are you calculating payback on gross profit, or are you quietly using revenue, and does that difference change your answer?

definitions.def: Customer Acquisition Cost, what you spend on average to gain one new paying customer.
How long it takes to recover what you spent acquiring a customer.

content.equation_example: CAC $150, Monthly Gross Profit per Acquired Customer $50. $150 divided by $50 equals 3 months. Lower means you recover your acquisition spend fast and can reinvest sooner; higher means your cash is tied up longer and a slow month puts more strain on you.
content.closing_statement: Fast payback buys you the freedom to keep betting.

caption:
Principle Nº 53: Payback-Period Architecture

Growth quality depends on how fast acquisition spend returns as gross profit.

Ask yourself: What's your business's target CAC payback, and are you on track to hit it at your market launch?
